article_meta.title: Media Assistant AG puts Don Lemon 'on notice' over Minnesota church protest

reasoning: Classification (Center) is reasonable for a largely neutral, procedural Hill report, but the response commits multiple attribution-rule violations in key_indicators by citing quoted/attributed source language. Confidence (0.9) is somewhat high for subtle/mostly balanced coverage.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Article reads as balanced procedural reporting with both parties’ positions and GOP intraparty splits; Center is appropriate.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.9 suggests unambiguous neutrality; a slightly lower value would better reflect subtle framing choices (e.g., author’s use of ‘voting reforms’).
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Several key_indicators improperly rely on quoted/attributed language (Thune and Schumer quotes; ‘Senate aides said’), violating the attribution rule.
